alerts.title: Israeli strike kills 12 in eastern Lebanon village as IDF calls up additional troops
alerts.summary: NBC News and Euronews report an Israeli strike on a village in eastern Lebanon (~34.0°N, 36.0°E) killed at least 12 people, with the IDF simultaneously mobilizing additional reservists for the Lebanon front. Middle East Eye reports parallel strikes on Kafr Sir in southern Lebanon and an attack near Lebanon's largest dam targeting an ambulance team, marking a sharp escalation alongside the US-Iran kinetic track.

alerts.title: Russian MFA declares start of 'systematic bombing' campaign against Kyiv
alerts.summary: Rybar's 26 May morning sitrep (~155K views) reports the Russian Foreign Ministry has announced the start of systematic strikes on infrastructure in Kyiv Oblast and called on foreign nationals to leave the region. Rybar separately notes Ukrainian negotiators are now requesting only 5–10 air-defense interceptors per tranche, citing shortages after US PAC-3 stockpile diversion to the Iran war.

alerts.title: Iran formally accuses US of 'gross violation' of ceasefire after fresh strikes
alerts.summary: BBC, Yahoo and Al Jazeera report Tehran has officially condemned the new wave of US strikes on southern Iran as a 'gross violation' of the standing ceasefire, with Al Jazeera asking openly whether the truce is over. The Iranian protest comes as delegations remain in Doha and as NYT confirms the strikes hit Iran's Gulf coast (~32°N, 53°E) during active negotiations.

alerts.title: Israel claims it destroyed Iran's cyber warfare headquarters
alerts.summary: Politico, surfaced via global_metrics cyber headlines, reports Israel says it has knocked out Iran's central cyber warfare headquarters. The claim arrives amid ongoing Iran-linked hacker harassment of US service members and CENTCOM's deployment of a defensive tech 'umbrella' over Strait of Hormuz shipping (DefenseScoop), suggesting a deliberate Israeli move to degrade Iranian retaliatory cyber capacity during the Doha track.

alerts.title: NATO rehearsed deep strikes into Russia in 'Arcade Strike' exercise from London bunker
alerts.summary: DeepState UA (~227K views) reports NATO's Allied Rapid Reaction Corps (ARRC) ran Operation Arcade Strike from a mothballed platform at Charing Cross Underground station, simulating deep strikes on Russian territory in response to a hypothetical Russian attack on a Baltic state. The exercise included EW, comms-suppression and counter-UAS drills under a modeled Baltic invasion scenario.

analysis_note: The US-Iran ceasefire is fraying in real time — fresh CENTCOM strikes on Iran's Gulf coast during the Doha talks have drawn a formal Iranian violation charge, while a parallel Israeli escalation in Lebanon (12 killed, reservists called up) and a declared Russian 'systematic' bombing campaign against Kyiv point to simultaneous escalation on three theaters at threat level 9.
